_provenance: AUTHORED source of truth for the book's headline numbers. Referenced from chapter prose via {{token}} substitution in build_book_html.py. A later pass refreshes the repo-derived figures from history-mining; the cost-model and policy figures are the book's canonical estimates. Edit here, never in the prose.
_loc_provenance: LoC tokens (prod_loc, support_loc, iac_loc, system_model_loc, total_loc, support_ratio) are POINT-IN-TIME source-tree category counts measured 2026-07-23 on /Users/davisjam/Projects/ada-tool (parent repo, HEAD at measurement) via the categorized cloc tool: `python3 tools/dev/run-cloc.py --json`. Category map: prod_loc=Production Code (490,231); support_loc=support-apparatus rollup (1,420,335 = everything except production; support_ratio 2.9 = support_loc/prod_loc); iac_loc=Infrastructure as Code (8,446); system_model_loc=System-model meta-files (7,380); total_loc=whole-tree code (2,593,580). These are source-tree counts, NOT the 15.5M engineering-line churn figure from repo-activity-histogram.py (which measures added+deleted lines over time).
_commit_provenance: Commit tokens (commits_total, commits_per_week, commits_per_day, peak_week_commits, study_weeks) from `python3 talks-and-notes/history-mining/repo-activity-histogram.py --metric commits` on the parent repo, measured 2026-07-23 (19 full weeks, 2026-03-12 -> 2026-07-16). commits_total=22,024 over 19 weeks; sustained rate ~200 commits/day (~1,000-1,400/week); peak_week_commits=3,329 (week of 2026-05-21). commits_per_day was corrected 500 -> 200 (500/day implied ~3,500/week, a rate only the single peak week approached).
_model_count_note: The Model Zoo census: 7 general model types (G1-G7) + 19 real typed system-models (M1-M19) + 3 split-out sub-models (M18a, M18b, M14a) = 29 named models. Presented as EVIDENCE for the 4+1 thesis (a zoo large enough that one view is never enough), not as a target to hit.
